creator: dbaio@freebsd.org
text:
Hello.

This broke misc/freebsd-doc-en and changed a few things, like the language's config, which impacts our documentation.

https://pkg-status.freebsd.org/beefy18/data/main-amd64-default/p7677d0cb38c5_s4f2cc73f34/logs/en-freebsd-doc-20230512,1.log

hugo v0.112.3+extended freebsd/amd64 BuildDate=2023-05-27T01:28:15Z-0300 VendorInfo=freebsd
WARN 2023/06/01 20:41:26 .Language.Params is deprecated and will be removed in a future release. .Language.Params is deprecated and will be removed in a future release. Use site.Params instead.

- For all but custom parameters, you need to use the built in Hugo variables, e.g. site.Title, site.LanguageCode; site.Language.Params.Title will not work.
- All custom parameters needs to be placed below params, e.g. [languages.en.params] in TOML.

See https://gohugo.io/content-management/multilingual/#changes-in-hugo-01120

Re-run Hugo with the flag --panicOnWarning to get a better error message.

--

And this is not working anymore.
$ DOC_LANG="en" make
...
HUGO_DISABLELANGUAGES="bn-bd da de el es fr hu id it ja ko mn nl pl pt-br ru tr zh-cn zh-tw" /usr/local/bin/hugo --minify

--


I've just tried the last version, hugo v0.112.6, and the same happens.

So, could you revert this upgrade for now? We will need more time to investigate this.

I wonder if you can pass this maintainership to doceng@ as it impacts the website and documentation portal.

Regards.
